mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) directly signals strengthening consumer demand, which increases corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 higher over short horizons.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return prediction based on RSAFS direction has Rank IC below 0.05 over any consecutive 2-year period in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a threshold of 7.0% signals a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which supports higher equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE drops below 7.0%, the S&P 500 60-day return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) compared to random periods, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rate combined with low inflation creates an accommodative monetary environment that compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, leading to positive forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 2.0% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year below 3.0%
failure_condition: If when the trigger condition holds, the 20-day SPX return is not positive in more than 55% of instances or the average return is not above 0.5%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate improving construction activity and household wealth, which boosts economic momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: In the post-recession regime, rising HOUST (3-month change > 0) predicts positive 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC below 0.05 over walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerating at a monthly rate above 0.5% signals robust manufacturing activity and economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings and rising equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%, the 20-day SPX return is not positive in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in both employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate a virtuous cycle of rising income and consumption, driving strong short-term equity market gains.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and RSAFS show positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: If when both PAYEMS and RSAFS grow month-over-month, the 5-day SPX return is not positive in more than 55% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) over a quarter signals a healthy labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to upward S&P 500 momentum over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return prediction based on PAYEMS direction (3-month change) has a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above the 0.5% threshold signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle, which increases discount rates and borrowing costs, exerting downward pressure on equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%, the 20-day SPX return is not negative in more than 50% of cases or the average return is not negative, the hypothesis is rejected.
